# Open-Meteo - Data Agreement

## Provider
Open-Meteo (open source project by Patrick Zippenfenig)

## URL
https://open-meteo.com/

## Terms of Service URL
https://open-meteo.com/en/terms

## License
CC BY 4.0 (Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International) for data. API source code is AGPL-3.0.

## Data We Collect
Weather data including current conditions, hourly/daily forecasts, historical weather, air quality indices, and marine weather via the Open-Meteo API. Data is sourced from national weather services (NOAA, DWD, MeteoFrance, etc.).

## How We Use It
Environmental data signal in DRM3 products. Weather data provides geographic and temporal context for enrichment and analysis. Used in composite signals that incorporate environmental conditions.

## Commercial Use
Yes. CC BY 4.0 permits commercial use. Open-Meteo's terms explicitly allow non-commercial use for free; commercial use requires attribution and, for high-volume use, a paid subscription to support the project.

## Redistribution
Yes, with attribution. CC BY 4.0 permits redistribution in any form provided attribution is given. We may serve weather data via our API as part of enriched data profiles.

## Attribution Required
Yes. CC BY 4.0 requires attribution. Must credit: "Weather data by Open-Meteo.com" with a link to https://open-meteo.com/. Must also credit the underlying national weather services where applicable.

## Rate Limits
- Free tier: 10,000 API calls per day, 5,000 per hour, 600 per minute
- No API key required for free tier
- Commercial/high-volume use: paid plans with higher limits

## Key Restrictions
- Must provide CC BY 4.0 attribution
- Commercial use at scale may require a paid subscription (not legally required by CC BY 4.0, but requested by the project)
- Must not overload the free API beyond documented limits
- Cannot sublicense the data under more restrictive terms

## Last Reviewed
2026-04-02
